events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, Brian May, was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar Legend
angles.copy: Celebrating the astronomical talent of Sir Brian May, who turns 79 today. From Queen's iconic riffs to his work as an astrophysicist, he truly is a star.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' iconic frontman, Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Enduring Rock Star
angles.copy: Still got the moves like Jagger! The one and only Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday today. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: MTV, originally Music Television, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast.

angles.name: Music TV Revolution
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV changed music forever. Remember the first video played? 'Video Killed The Radio Star' kicked off a new era for artists and fans.

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became the fastest-selling debut album in British history at the time.

angles.name: Britpop Landmark
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and Britpop was never the same. An absolute game-changer. What's your top track from this album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The iconic Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. A legendary festival that defined a generation.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death (1977)
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the King of Rock and Roll, passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day in 1977, the world lost a legend. Remembering Elvis Presley, whose music continues to inspire generations. Long live the King.

events.title: Tupac Shakur shot in Las Vegas (1996)
events.blurb: Hip-hop icon Tupac Shakur was critically wounded in a drive-by shooting in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and tragically died six days later.

angles.name: Hip-Hop Icon
angles.copy: Thirty years ago today, the music world was rocked by the shooting of Tupac Shakur in Las Vegas. His legacy in hip-hop remains undeniable.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death (1970)
events.blurb: Legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix passed away on September 18, 1970, leaving an indelible mark on music history.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who left us on this day in 1970. His revolutionary guitar work still sounds as fresh and powerful as ever.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover, those tracks – pure magic. What's your favourite from this masterpiece?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman, Chrissie Hynde, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock & roll icon with a voice that commands attention.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis co-founder and former frontman, Liam Gallagher, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Voice of a Generation
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that swagger and those anthems. What's your favourite track he's sung?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' single re-released in UK (1975)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was re-released in the UK on October 24, 1975, from his 'Shaved Fish' compilation.

angles.name: Timeless Anthem
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since its original release, and 49 years since its UK single re-release, John Lennon's 'Imagine' remains a powerful message for the world.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple introduced the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? Twenty-five years ago, Apple dropped a game-changer that put '1,000 songs in your pocket' and redefined portable music.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th marks 'James Bond Day', celebrating the premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007, the enduring British icon who always delivers. What's your favourite Bond theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles co-founder, John Lennon, was born on October 9, 1940.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday today. His music and message of peace continue to resonate globally.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: English actor, comedian, and writer John Cleese, best known for Monty Python and Fawlty Towers, was born on October 27, 1939.

angles.name: Comedy Genius
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the truly iconic John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, he's given us endless laughs. What's your favourite Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' (1968)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: The White Album
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago today, The Beatles dropped their sprawling, iconic 'White Album'. A double LP packed with diverse sounds and timeless tracks.
